comorbidities: Hypertension Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Hyperlipidemia Former smoker (35 pack-years, quit 8 years ago)
medications: Amlodipine 5 mg daily Metformin 1000 mg twice daily Atorvastatin 20 mg nightly Aspirin 81 mg daily
pending_tests:
Next-generation sequencing (NGS) comprehensive panel
Pulmonary function testing (PFT)
Pre-operative cardiac clearance
prior_treatment: No prior systemic therapy No prior radiation therapy Diagnostic biopsy only
notes: Patient is a 62-year-old woman with newly diagnosed stage IIA lung adenocarcinoma localized to the right upper lobe. Performance status remains good (ECOG 1). Multidisciplinary tumor board reviewed imaging and pathology findings. Disease appears surgically resectable. Pending completion of pulmonary function testing and cardiac evaluation. If deemed an acceptable surgical candidate, plan for right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node sampling followed by consideration of adjuvant therapy based on final pathology and molecular profiling results.
intake_text: The patient presented with a three-month history of persistent dry cough and intermittent right-sided chest discomfort. She denied hemoptysis, significant weight loss, fever, or night sweats. Initial chest radiograph revealed a right upper lobe opacity, prompting CT imaging. Subsequent biopsy confirmed lung adenocarcinoma. PET-CT demonstrated localized disease without evidence of nodal involvement or distant metastasis. The patient remains independent in activities of daily living and reports mild fatigue but continues normal household activities. She is motivated to pursue curative-intent treatment and has strong family support. Current focus is completion of pre-operative evaluation and review of comprehensive molecular testing results.

diagnosis: HER2-Positive Invasive Ductal Carcinoma, Stage IIIA
imaging: Mammogram: Mass with biopsy-proven HER2 positivity. Breast MRI: Enhancing mass with axillary lymph node involvement. PET/CT: No distant metastases.
pathology: Core biopsy: Invasive Ductal Carcinoma, HER2-positive (IHC 3+). Axillary lymph node biopsy: Invasive Ductal Carcinoma, HER2-positive (IHC 3+).
pending_tests: Pathology: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) for Ki-67. Imaging: Follow-up PET/CT.
notes: Patient presents with palpable breast mass and axillary lymph node involvement. Imaging confirms Stage IIIA disease. Biopsy confirms HER2-positive invasive ductal carcinoma. Patient received neoadjuvant chemotherapy and HER2-targeted therapy. Treatment response assessment is pending.
intake_text: Patient is a 47-year-old female with a newly diagnosed HER2-positive invasive ductal carcinoma, Stage IIIA. Biopsy confirms HER2 positivity and ER/PR positivity. Imaging shows a breast mass and axillary lymph node involvement. She received neoadjuvant chemotherapy and HER2-targeted therapy. The multidisciplinary team will discuss treatment options, including potential surgical resection and adjuvant therapy.

imaging: PET/CT shows multiple liver lesions and bone metastases.
pathology: Core biopsy confirms invasive ductal carcinoma.
pending_tests: Bone scan; Surgical consultation for liver lesion biopsy
notes: Patient presents with symptoms of fatigue and weight loss. Further evaluation required.
intake_text: New patient presenting with metastatic breast cancer. Initial workup confirms stage IV disease. Discussed treatment options including chemotherapy and targeted therapy. Patient declined initial chemotherapy recommendation.

imaging: Chest X-ray: Clear. CT Abdomen/Pelvis: No lymphadenopathy.
pathology: Bone marrow biopsy: Hypercellular marrow with >20% blasts, consistent with AML. Cytogenetics: Trisomy 8, del(15q).
pending_tests: Flow cytometry for CD33, CD123; FISH for RUNX1T1, CEBPA
notes: Patient presents with fatigue and fever. Initial CBC shows pancytopenia. Peripheral smear shows blasts.
intake_text: New onset AML requiring initiation of chemotherapy. Patient is hemodynamically stable. Discussed treatment options including 7+3 regimen.

imaging: PET/CT shows extensive nodal involvement with minimal extranodal disease.
pathology: Core biopsy confirms DLBCL, GCB subtype.
pending_tests: Bone marrow biopsy; CT scan of chest
notes: Patient presents with rapidly enlarging left axillary mass. Initial biopsy confirms DLBCL. Pending further workup.
intake_text: 35-year-old female with newly diagnosed Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma. Presents with rapidly enlarging left axillary mass. Initial biopsy confirms DLBCL, GCB subtype. Pending further workup.
